steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses joined by the transition word "however," which signals a contrast. Standard English requires a period (not a comma) between two independent clauses when a conjunctive adverb like "however" appears at the boundary.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — however. All: The period ends the first independent clause; "however" then opens the contrasting second sentence. This is the correct pattern. ✓
  • Choice B — however all: A comma between two independent clauses creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — however, all: Still leaves the two independent clauses joined without a period, producing a run-on. ✗
  • Choice D — however and all: "And" signals addition, not contrast, so it contradicts the meaning signaled by "however." ✗
That matches Choice A — however. All.
